results.description: MARC AT THE UNIVERSITY OF KANSAS - PROJECT SUMMARY/ABSTRACT  THE UNIVERSITY OF KANSAS (KU) REQUESTS RENEWAL OF ITS MAXIMIZING ACCESS TO RESEARCH CAREERS (KU MARC). KU IS A R1 RESEARCH INTENSIVE INSTITUTION, A MEMBER OF THE ASSOCIATION OF AMERICAN UNIVERSITIES (AAU), AND THE KU OFFICE FOR DIVERSITY IN SCIENCE TRAINING ADMINISTERS A BROAD PORTFOLIO OF RESEARCH TRAINING PROGRAMS. KU MARC SEEKS TO TRAIN 30 SCHOLARS OVER FIVE YEARS, PREPARE THEM TO BECOME LEADERS IN THE BIOMEDICAL WORKFORCE, AND WILL PLACE SPECIAL EMPHASIS ON INCREASING THE NUMBER OF AMERICAN INDIAN AND ALASKA NATIVE (AI/AN) SCHOLARS CONDUCTING DOCTORAL-LEVEL RESEARCH. SIX STUDENTS PER YEAR WILL JOIN OUR PROGRAM AS RISING JUNIORS AND EXPERIENCE A FULL TWO YEARS IN THE MARC PROGRAM. WE WILL PLACE AT LEAST 70% OF THESE SCHOLARS INTO A BIOMEDICAL PHD OR MD/PHD PROGRAM WITHIN THREE YEARS OF COMPLETING THE PROGRAM. KU MARC AND ITS PREDECESSOR, KU IMSD, HAVE A HIGHLY SUCCESSFUL HISTORY OF RECRUITING A DIVERSE GROUP OF SCHOLARS, AND WE ARE UNIQUELY POSITIONED TO RECRUIT AND TRAIN AI/AN SCHOLARS, DUE TO LONG-STANDING COLLABORATIONS BETWEEN KU AND HASKELL INDIAN NATIONS UNIVERSITY (HINU), INSTITUTIONS LOCATED LESS THAN 2 MILES APART IN LAWRENCE, KS.  THE ACTIVITIES OF KU MARC SCHOLARS ARE SHAPED BY A COMPREHENSIVE INDIVIDUAL DEVELOPMENT PLAN (IDP), WHICH IS CONTINUALLY RE-EVALUATED AND ADJUSTED BY THE SCHOLARS AND THE KU MARC MENTORING TEAM. RESEARCH TRAINING IS A CENTRAL FOCUS OF THE IDP, AND EACH SCHOLAR COMPLETES A RESEARCH PROJECT IN A BIOMEDICALLY-FOCUSED LABORATORY. THIS EXPERIENCE DEVELOPS THE SCHOLAR’S ABILITY TO CREATE AND TEST SCIENTIFIC HYPOTHESES AND OBTAIN THE SKILLS NECESSARY TO CONDUCT RIGOROUS, INDEPENDENT, REPRODUCIBLE BIOMEDICAL RESEARCH. CAREFUL SELECTION OF PARTICIPATING FACULTY, SUBSTANTIVE TRAINING OF THESE MENTORS, AND ROBUST MENTOR EVALUATION ENSURES THAT SCHOLARS ARE PROVIDED WITH SUPPORTIVE AND CULTURALLY-RESPONSIVE MENTORING. SCHOLARS PRESENT THEIR WORK AT THE ANNUAL HINU-KU STUDENT RESEARCH SYMPOSIUM, AND ALSO GIVE A PRESENTATION AT A NATIONAL CONFERENCE. ADVANCED COURSEWORK BUILDS FOUNDATIONAL KNOWLEDGE IN PREPARATION FOR GRADUATE SCHOOL. OUR PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT SERIES FOCUSES ON PROMOTING SCHOLARS’ WELL-BEING (E.G., BUILDING RESILIENCE, ADDRESSING CHALLENGES FACED BY SCIENTISTS FROM UNDERREPRESENTED GROUPS), HELPING THEM MATURE AS PROFESSIONALS WHILE MAINTAINING THEIR CULTURAL IDENTITIES, BUILDING COMMUNICATION SKILLS, AND INCREASING THEIR SELF-EFFICACY. OUR MENTORING TEAM WORKS CLOSELY WITH SCHOLARS TO ESTABLISH AND STRENGTHEN A SUPPORT NETWORK THAT THEY CAN UTILIZE THROUGHOUT THEIR CAREER. THIS NETWORK INCLUDES THE SCHOLAR’S FACULTY MENTOR, OTHER RESEARCH COLLABORATORS, THEIR PEERS, KU MARC ALUMNI AND THE KU MARC LEADERSHIP TEAM. REGULAR PROGRAM EVALUATIONS WILL ALLOW KU MARC TO BUILD ON OUR PRIOR SUCCESSES, ADDRESS ANY CHALLENGES, AND CONTINUOUSLY IMPROVE AND INNOVATE IN OUR EFFORTS TO MEET THE GOAL OF DIVERSIFYING THE BIOMEDICAL RESEARCH WORKFORCE.

OUR PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT SERIES FOCUSES ON PROMOTING SCHOLARS’ WELL-BEING (E.G., BUILDING RESILIENCE, ADDRESSING CHALLENGES FACED BY SCIENTISTS FROM UNDERREPRESENTED GROUPS), HELPING THEM MATURE AS PROFESSIONALS WHILE MAINTAINING THEIR CULTURAL IDENTITIES, BUILDING COMMUNICATION SKILLS, AND INCREASING THEIR SELF-EFFICACY. OUR MENTORING TEAM WORKS CLOSELY WITH SCHOLARS TO ESTABLISH AND STRENGTHEN A SUPPORT NETWORK THAT THEY CAN UTILIZE THROUGHOUT THEIR CAREER. THIS NETWORK INCLUDES THE SCHOLAR’S FACULTY MENTOR, OTHER RESEARCH COLLABORATORS, THEIR PEERS, KU MARC ALUMNI AND THE KU MARC LEADERSHIP TEAM. REGULAR PROGRAM EVALUATIONS WILL ALLOW KU MARC TO BUILD ON OUR PRIOR SUCCESSES, ADDRESS ANY CHALLENGES, AND CONTINUOUSLY IMPROVE AND INNOVATE IN OUR EFFORTS TO MEET THE GOAL OF DIVERSIFYING THE BIOMEDICAL RESEARCH WORKFORCE.

MARC AT THE UNIVERSITY OF KANSAS - SUMMARY THE CENTRAL GOAL OF THE UNIVERSITY OF KANSAS’ MULTIDISCIPLINARY MAXIMIZING ACCESS TO RESEARCH CAREERS PROGRAM (KU MARC) IS TO PROVIDE RESEARCH, ACADEMIC, AND PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT TRAINING TO STUDENTS FROM GROUPS UNDERREPRESENTED IN THE BIOMEDICAL SCIENCES. THE PROGRAM WILL HAVE A SPECIAL EMPHASIS ON AMERICAN INDIAN (AI) STUDENTS. ALL PARTICIPANT STUDENTS WILL BE WELL PREPARED TO THRIVE IN COMPETITIVE PHD PROGRAMS AND ASSUME LEADERSHIP ROLES IN SUBSEQUENT BIOMEDICAL RESEARCH CAREERS. THE GEOGRAPHIC AND DEMOGRAPHIC CONTEXT IN WHICH KU IS SITUATED PROVIDES MANY UNIQUE OPPORTUNITIES AND CHALLENGES FOR THIS TRAINING PROGRAM. KU IS A PREMIER RESEARCH INSTITUTION, AN AAU MEMBER, AND CLASSIFIED BY THE CARNEGIE FOUNDATION AS A “DOCTORAL UNIVERSITY: VERY HIGH RESEARCH ACTIVITY”. GIVEN ITS SUBURBAN/RURAL LOCATION, KU IS A PREDOMINANTLY MAJORITY-SERVING INSTITUTION. HOWEVER, KU’S RECRUITMENT AND RETENTION EFFORTS TO INCREASE DIVERSITY ARE PAYING DIVIDENDS, AND 45% OF UNDERGRADUATES IN KU MARC-PARTICIPATING PROGRAMS ARE MARC-ELIGIBLE, PROVIDING A ROBUST APPLICANT POOL. MOREOVER, KU IS FORTUNATE TO HAVE A LONG-STANDING RELATIONSHIP WITH NEARBY HASKELL INDIAN NATIONS UNIVERSITY, INCLUDING A CO-SPONSORED BRIDGES TO THE BACCALAUREATE PROGRAM. KU MARC IS WELL-POSITIONED TO RECRUIT AND TRAIN AI STUDENTS, WHO ARE SEVERELY UNDERREPRESENTED IN THE SCIENCES, THROUGH ITS RESEARCH AND PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT TRAINING AS WELL AS ITS TUITION SUPPORT FOR STUDENTS TRANSFERRING FROM HASKELL TO KU. KU MARC’S PROGRAMMATIC ACTIVITIES ARE FOCUSED BOTH ON SCHOLARS’ DEVELOPMENT AS SCIENTISTS AND ON BUILDING AN INCLUSIVE COMMUNITY THAT CAN PROVIDE SUPPORT AS MARC SCHOLARS NAVIGATE THE MANY CHALLENGES INHERENT TO BEING UNDERREPRESENTED IN THEIR FIELDS. KEY ACTIVITIES WILL INCLUDE 1) A PRE-MARC PROGRAM, WHERE POTENTIAL MARC SCHOLARS WILL BE MENTORED BY CURRENT SCHOLARS AND WILL EXPLORE BIOMEDICAL RESEARCH AND CAREER OPPORTUNITIES; 2) INDIVIDUAL DEVELOPMENT PLANS TO GUIDE SCHOLARS’ RESEARCH, ACADEMIC, AND PROFESSIONAL ACTIVITIES; AND 3) COMPREHENSIVE RESEARCH EXPERIENCES (10-15 HRS/WK DURING THE ACADEMIC YEAR; 40 HRS/WK IN SUMMER, INCLUDING A REQUIRED OFF-CAMPUS RESEARCH EXPERIENCE) UNDER THE GUIDANCE OF A KU FACULTY MENTOR. RESEARCH EXPERIENCES WILL BE FOCUSED ON CULTIVATING SCHOLARS’ ABILITY TO DEVELOP HYPOTHESES, DESIGN RIGOROUS AND UNBIASED EXPERIMENTS, ANALYZE AND INTERPRET RESULTS, AND DISSEMINATE FINDINGS WIDELY. KEY ACTIVITIES ALSO INCLUDE 4) RESPONSIBLE CONDUCT OF RESEARCH AND RIGOR AND REPRODUCIBILITY TRAINING; 5) COURSEWORK INCLUDING SCIENTIFIC WRITING, QUANTITATIVE/ANALYTICAL METHODS, AND HOW TO PREPARE COMPETITIVE GRADUATE SCHOOL APPLICATIONS; 6) MATCHING OF NEW MARC SCHOLARS WITH IMSD/MARC ALUMNI MENTORS TO PROVIDE SUPPORT AND GUIDANCE; 7) OPPORTUNITIES TO PRESENT RESEARCH AT NATIONAL, REGIONAL AND LOCAL CONFERENCES; AND 8) LEADERSHIP AND OUTREACH OPPORTUNITIES HELD IN COLLABORATION WITH KU MARC’S MANY CAMPUS PARTNERS. WE PROPOSE TO TRAIN 24 MARC SCHOLARS OVER THE NEXT FIVE YEARS, WITH A PARTICULAR EMPHASIS ON TRAINING AI STUDENTS, AS MENTIONED ABOVE. OUR GOAL IS THAT MORE THAN 70% OF SCHOLARS WILL TRANSITION INTO BIOMEDICAL PHD/MD-PHD PROGRAMS.

MARC AT THE UNIVERSITY OF KANSAS - SUMMARY THE CENTRAL GOAL OF THE UNIVERSITY OF KANSAS’ MULTIDISCIPLINARY MAXIMIZING ACCESS TO RESEARCH CAREERS PROGRAM (KU MARC) IS TO PROVIDE RESEARCH, ACADEMIC, AND PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT TRAINING TO STUDENTS FROM GROUPS UNDERREPRESENTED IN THE BIOMEDICAL SCIENCES. THE PROGRAM WILL HAVE A SPECIAL EMPHASIS ON AMERICAN INDIAN (AI) STUDENTS. ALL PARTICIPANT STUDENTS WILL BE WELL PREPARED TO THRIVE IN COMPETITIVE PHD PROGRAMS AND ASSUME LEADERSHIP ROLES IN SUBSEQUENT BIOMEDICAL RESEARCH CAREERS. THE GEOGRAPHIC AND DEMOGRAPHIC CONTEXT IN WHICH KU IS SITUATED PROVIDES MANY UNIQUE OPPORTUNITIES AND CHALLENGES FOR THIS TRAINING PROGRAM. KU IS A PREMIER RESEARCH INSTITUTION, AN AAU MEMBER, AND CLASSIFIED BY THE CARNEGIE FOUNDATION AS A “DOCTORAL UNIVERSITY: VERY HIGH RESEARCH ACTIVITY”. GIVEN ITS SUBURBAN/RURAL LOCATION, KU IS A PREDOMINANTLY MAJORITY-SERVING INSTITUTION. HOWEVER, KU’S RECRUITMENT AND RETENTION EFFORTS TO INCREASE DIVERSITY ARE PAYING DIVIDENDS, AND 45% OF UNDERGRADUATES IN KU MARC-PARTICIPATING PROGRAMS ARE MARC-ELIGIBLE, PROVIDING A ROBUST APPLICANT POOL. MOREOVER, KU IS FORTUNATE TO HAVE A LONG-STANDING RELATIONSHIP WITH NEARBY HASKELL INDIAN NATIONS UNIVERSITY, INCLUDING A CO-SPONSORED BRIDGES TO THE BACCALAUREATE PROGRAM. KU MARC IS WELL-POSITIONED TO RECRUIT AND TRAIN AI STUDENTS, WHO ARE SEVERELY UNDERREPRESENTED IN THE SCIENCES, THROUGH ITS RESEARCH AND PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT TRAINING AS WELL AS ITS TUITION SUPPORT FOR STUDENTS TRANSFERRING FROM HASKELL TO KU. KU MARC’S PROGRAMMATIC ACTIVITIES ARE FOCUSED BOTH ON SCHOLARS’ DEVELOPMENT AS SCIENTISTS AND ON BUILDING AN INCLUSIVE COMMUNITY THAT CAN PROVIDE SUPPORT AS MARC SCHOLARS NAVIGATE THE MANY CHALLENGES INHERENT TO BEING UNDERREPRESENTED IN THEIR FIELDS. KEY ACTIVITIES WILL INCLUDE 1) A PRE-MARC PROGRAM, WHERE POTENTIAL MARC SCHOLARS WILL BE MENTORED BY CURRENT SCHOLARS AND WILL EXPLORE BIOMEDICAL RESEARCH AND CAREER OPPORTUNITIES; 2) INDIVIDUAL DEVELOPMENT PLANS TO GUIDE SCHOLARS’ RESEARCH, ACADEMIC, AND PROFESSIONAL ACTIVITIES; AND 3) COMPREHENSIVE RESEARCH EXPERIENCES (10-15 HRS/WK DURING THE ACADEMIC YEAR; 40 HRS/WK IN SUMMER, INCLUDING A REQUIRED OFF-CAMPUS RESEARCH EXPERIENCE) UNDER THE GUIDANCE OF A KU FACULTY MENTOR. RESEARCH EXPERIENCES WILL BE FOCUSED ON CULTIVATING SCHOLARS’ ABILITY TO DEVELOP HYPOTHESES, DESIGN RIGOROUS AND UNBIASED EXPERIMENTS, ANALYZE AND INTERPRET RESULTS, AND DISSEMINATE FINDINGS WIDELY. KEY ACTIVITIES ALSO INCLUDE 4) RESPONSIBLE CONDUCT OF RESEARCH AND RIGOR AND REPRODUCIBILITY TRAINING; 5) COURSEWORK INCLUDING SCIENTIFIC WRITING, QUANTITATIVE/ANALYTICAL METHODS, AND HOW TO PREPARE COMPETITIVE GRADUATE SCHOOL APPLICATIONS; 6) MATCHING OF NEW MARC SCHOLARS WITH IMSD/MARC ALUMNI MENTORS TO PROVIDE SUPPORT AND GUIDANCE; 7) OPPORTUNITIES TO PRESENT RESEARCH AT NATIONAL, REGIONAL AND LOCAL CONFERENCES; AND 8) LEADERSHIP AND OUTREACH OPPORTUNITIES HELD IN COLLABORATION WITH KU MARC’S MANY CAMPUS PARTNERS. WE PROPOSE TO TRAIN 24 MARC SCHOLARS OVER THE NEXT FIVE YEARS, WITH A PARTICULAR EMPHASIS ON TRAINING AI STUDENTS, AS MENTIONED ABOVE. OUR GOAL IS THAT MORE THAN 70% OF SCHOLARS WILL TRANSITION INTO BIOMEDICAL PHD/MD-PHD PROGRAMS.
